class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.calculation
documentation: A string describing the task to be performed

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.title
documentation: reprinted on output.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.verbosity
documentation: Currently two verbosity levels are implemented: high, low. 'debug' and 'medium' have the same effect as 'high'; 'default' and 'minimal' as 'low'

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.wf_collect
documentation: OBSOLETE - NO LONGER IMPLEMENTED

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.nstep
documentation: Default: 1 if calculation == 'scf', 'nscf', 'bands'; 50 for the other cases; Number of molecular-dynamics or structural optimization steps performed in this run. If set to 0, the code performs a quick "dry run", stopping just after initialization. This is useful to check for input correctness and to have the summary printed. NOTE: in MD calculations, the code will perform "nstep" steps even if restarting from a previously interrupted calculation.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.iprint
documentation: band energies are written every iprint iterations

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.tstress
documentation: calculate stress. It is set to .TRUE. automatically if calculation == 'vc-md' or 'vc-relax'

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.tprnfor
documentation: calculate forces. It is set to .TRUE. automatically if calculation == 'relax','md','vc-md'

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.dt
documentation: time step for molecular dynamics, in Rydberg atomic units (1 a.u.=4.8378 * 10^-17 s : beware, the CP code uses Hartree atomic units, half that much!!!)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.outdir
documentation: input, temporary, output files are found in this directory, see also wfcdir

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.wfcdir
documentation: This directory specifies where to store files generated by each processor (*.wfc{N}, *.igk{N}, etc.). Useful for machines without a parallel file system: set wfcdir to a local file system, while outdir should be a parallel or network file system, visible to all processors. Beware: in order to restart from interrupted runs, or to perform further calculations using the produced data files, you may need to copy files to outdir. Works only for pw.x.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.prefix
documentation: prepended to input/output filenames: prefix.wfc, prefix.rho, etc.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.lkpoint_dir
documentation: OBSOLETE - NO LONGER IMPLEMENTED

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.max_seconds
documentation: Jobs stops after max_seconds CPU time. Use this option in conjunction with option restart_mode if you need to split a job too long to complete into shorter jobs that fit into your batch queues.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.etot_conv_thr
documentation: Convergence threshold on total energy (a.u) for ionic minimization: the convergence criterion is satisfied when the total energy changes less than etot_conv_thr between two consecutive scf steps. Note that etot_conv_thr is extensive, like the total energy. See also forc_conv_thr - both criteria must be satisfied

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.forc_conv_thr
documentation: Convergence threshold on forces (a.u) for ionic minimization: the convergence criterion is satisfied when all components of all forces are smaller than forc_conv_thr. See also etot_conv_thr - both criteria must be satisfied

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.disk_io
documentation: Specifies the amount of disk I/O activity: (only for binary files and xml data file in data directory; other files printed at each molecular dynamics / structural optimization step are not controlled by this option )

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.pseudo_dir
documentation: directory containing pseudopotential files. Default: value of the $ESPRESSO_PSEUDO environment variable if set; '$HOME/espresso/pseudo/' otherwise

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.tefield
documentation: If .TRUE. a saw-like potential simulating an electric field is added to the bare ionic potential. See variables edir, eamp, emaxpos, eopreg for the form and size of the added potential.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.dipfield
documentation: If .TRUE. and tefield==.TRUE. a dipole correction is also added to the bare ionic potential - implements the recipe of L. Bengtsson, PRB 59, 12301 (1999). See variables edir, emaxpos, eopreg for the form of the correction. Must be used ONLY in a slab geometry, for surface calculations, with the discontinuity FALLING IN THE EMPTY SPACE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.lelfield
documentation: If .TRUE. a homogeneous finite electric field described through the modern theory of the polarization is applied. This is different from tefield == .true. !

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.nberrycyc
documentation: In the case of a finite electric field  ( lelfield == .TRUE. ) it defines the number of iterations for converging the wavefunctions in the electric field Hamiltonian, for each external iteration on the charge density

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.lorbm
documentation: If .TRUE. perform orbital magnetization calculation.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.lberry
documentation: If .TRUE. perform a Berry phase calculation. See the header of PW/src/bp_c_phase.f90 for documentation

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.gdir
documentation: For Berry phase calculation: direction of the k-point strings in reciprocal space. Allowed values: 1, 2, 3 1=first, 2=second, 3=third reciprocal lattice vector For calculations with finite electric fields (lelfield==.true.) "gdir" is the direction of the field.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.nppstr
documentation: For Berry phase calculation: number of k-points to be calculated along each symmetry-reduced string. The same for calculation with finite electric fields (lelfield==.true.).

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.gate
documentation: In the case of charged cells (tot_charge .ne. 0) setting gate = .TRUE. represents the counter charge (i.e. -tot_charge) not by a homogeneous background charge but with a charged plate, which is placed at zgate (see below). Details of the gate potential can be found in T. Brumme, M. Calandra, F. Mauri; PRB 89, 245406 (2014). Note, that in systems which are not symmetric with respect to the plate, one needs to enable the dipole correction! (dipfield=.true.). Currently, symmetry can be used with gate=.true. but carefully check that no symmetry is included which maps z to -z even if in principle one could still use them for symmetric systems (i.e. no dipole correction). For nosym=.false. verbosity is set to 'high'. Note: this option was called "monopole" in v6.0 and 6.1 of pw.x

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.twochem
documentation: IF .TRUE. , a two chemical potential calculation for the simulation of photoexcited systems is performed, constraining a fraction of the electrons in the conduction manifold.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.lfcp
documentation: If .TRUE. perform a constant bias potential (constant-mu) calculation for a system with ESM method. See the header of PW/src/fcp_module.f90 for documentation. To perform the calculation, you must set a namelist FCP.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.control.ControlSchema.trism
documentation: If .TRUE. perform a 3D-RISM-SCF calculation [for details see H.Sato et al., JCP 112, 9463 (2000), doi:10.1063/1.481564]. The solvent's distributions are calculated by 3D-RISM, though solute is treated as SCF. The charge density and the atomic positions are optimized, simultaneously with the solvents. To perform the calculation, you must set a namelist RISM and a card SOLVENTS. If assume_isolated = 'esm' and esm_bc = 'bc1', Laue-RISM is calculated instead of 3D-RISM and coupled with ESM method (i.e. ESM-RISM). [for details see S.Nishihara and M.Otani, PRB 96, 115429 (2017)]. The default of mixing_beta is 0.2 for both 3D-RISM and Laue-RISM. For structural relaxation with BFGS, ignore_wolfe is always .TRUE. .

documentation: Default: 1 if calculation == 'scf', 'nscf', 'bands'; 50 for the other cases; Number of molecular-dynamics or structural optimization steps performed in this run. If set to 0, the code performs a quick "dry run", stopping just after initialization. This is useful to check for input correctness and to have the summary printed. NOTE: in MD calculations, the code will perform "nstep" steps even if restarting from a previously interrupted calculation.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.tstress
documentation: calculate stress. It is set to .TRUE. automatically if calculation == 'vc-md' or 'vc-relax'

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.tprnfor
documentation: calculate forces. It is set to .TRUE. automatically if calculation == 'relax','md','vc-md'

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.dt
documentation: time step for molecular dynamics, in Rydberg atomic units (1 a.u.=4.8378 * 10^-17 s : beware, the CP code uses Hartree atomic units, half that much!!!)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.outdir
documentation: input, temporary, output files are found in this directory, see also wfcdir

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.wfcdir
documentation: This directory specifies where to store files generated by each processor (*.wfc{N}, *.igk{N}, etc.). Useful for machines without a parallel file system: set wfcdir to a local file system, while outdir should be a parallel or network file system, visible to all processors. Beware: in order to restart from interrupted runs, or to perform further calculations using the produced data files, you may need to copy files to outdir. Works only for pw.x.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.prefix
documentation: prepended to input/output filenames: prefix.wfc, prefix.rho, etc.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.lkpoint_dir
documentation: OBSOLETE - NO LONGER IMPLEMENTED

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.max_seconds
documentation: Jobs stops after max_seconds CPU time. Use this option in conjunction with option restart_mode if you need to split a job too long to complete into shorter jobs that fit into your batch queues.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.etot_conv_thr
documentation: Convergence threshold on total energy (a.u) for ionic minimization: the convergence criterion is satisfied when the total energy changes less than etot_conv_thr between two consecutive scf steps. Note that etot_conv_thr is extensive, like the total energy. See also forc_conv_thr - both criteria must be satisfied

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.forc_conv_thr
documentation: Convergence threshold on forces (a.u) for ionic minimization: the convergence criterion is satisfied when all components of all forces are smaller than forc_conv_thr. See also etot_conv_thr - both criteria must be satisfied

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.disk_io
documentation: Specifies the amount of disk I/O activity: (only for binary files and xml data file in data directory; other files printed at each molecular dynamics / structural optimization step are not controlled by this option )

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.pseudo_dir
documentation: directory containing pseudopotential files. Default: value of the $ESPRESSO_PSEUDO environment variable if set; '$HOME/espresso/pseudo/' otherwise

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.tefield
documentation: If .TRUE. a saw-like potential simulating an electric field is added to the bare ionic potential. See variables edir, eamp, emaxpos, eopreg for the form and size of the added potential.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.dipfield
documentation: If .TRUE. and tefield==.TRUE. a dipole correction is also added to the bare ionic potential - implements the recipe of L. Bengtsson, PRB 59, 12301 (1999). See variables edir, emaxpos, eopreg for the form of the correction. Must be used ONLY in a slab geometry, for surface calculations, with the discontinuity FALLING IN THE EMPTY SPACE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.lelfield
documentation: If .TRUE. a homogeneous finite electric field described through the modern theory of the polarization is applied. This is different from tefield == .true. !

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.nberrycyc
documentation: In the case of a finite electric field  ( lelfield == .TRUE. ) it defines the number of iterations for converging the wavefunctions in the electric field Hamiltonian, for each external iteration on the charge density

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.lorbm
documentation: If .TRUE. perform orbital magnetization calculation.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.lberry
documentation: If .TRUE. perform a Berry phase calculation. See the header of PW/src/bp_c_phase.f90 for documentation

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.gdir
documentation: For Berry phase calculation: direction of the k-point strings in reciprocal space. Allowed values: 1, 2, 3 1=first, 2=second, 3=third reciprocal lattice vector For calculations with finite electric fields (lelfield==.true.) "gdir" is the direction of the field.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.nppstr
documentation: For Berry phase calculation: number of k-points to be calculated along each symmetry-reduced string. The same for calculation with finite electric fields (lelfield==.true.).

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.gate
documentation: In the case of charged cells (tot_charge .ne. 0) setting gate = .TRUE. represents the counter charge (i.e. -tot_charge) not by a homogeneous background charge but with a charged plate, which is placed at zgate (see below). Details of the gate potential can be found in T. Brumme, M. Calandra, F. Mauri; PRB 89, 245406 (2014). Note, that in systems which are not symmetric with respect to the plate, one needs to enable the dipole correction! (dipfield=.true.). Currently, symmetry can be used with gate=.true. but carefully check that no symmetry is included which maps z to -z even if in principle one could still use them for symmetric systems (i.e. no dipole correction). For nosym=.false. verbosity is set to 'high'. Note: this option was called "monopole" in v6.0 and 6.1 of pw.x

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.twochem
documentation: IF .TRUE. , a two chemical potential calculation for the simulation of photoexcited systems is performed, constraining a fraction of the electrons in the conduction manifold.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.lfcp
documentation: If .TRUE. perform a constant bias potential (constant-mu) calculation for a system with ESM method. See the header of PW/src/fcp_module.f90 for documentation. To perform the calculation, you must set a namelist FCP.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ControlSchema.trism
documentation: If .TRUE. perform a 3D-RISM-SCF calculation [for details see H.Sato et al., JCP 112, 9463 (2000), doi:10.1063/1.481564]. The solvent's distributions are calculated by 3D-RISM, though solute is treated as SCF. The charge density and the atomic positions are optimized, simultaneously with the solvents. To perform the calculation, you must set a namelist RISM and a card SOLVENTS. If assume_isolated = 'esm' and esm_bc = 'bc1', Laue-RISM is calculated instead of 3D-RISM and coupled with ESM method (i.e. ESM-RISM). [for details see S.Nishihara and M.Otani, PRB 96, 115429 (2017)]. The default of mixing_beta is 0.2 for both 3D-RISM and Laue-RISM. For structural relaxation with BFGS, ignore_wolfe is always .TRUE. .

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.nat
documentation: number of atoms in the unit cell (ALL atoms, except if space_group is set, in which case, INEQUIVALENT atoms)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.ntyp
documentation: number of types of atoms in the unit cell

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.nbnd
documentation: Default: for an insulator, nbnd = number of valence bands (nbnd = # of electrons /2); for a metal, 20% more (minimum 4 more)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.nbnd_cond
documentation: Default: nbnd_cond = nbnd - # of electrons / 2 in the collinear case; nbnd_cond = nbnd - # of electrons in the noncollinear case.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.starting_charge
documentation: starting charge on atomic type 'i', to create starting potential with startingpot = 'atomic'.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.tot_magnetization
documentation: Total majority spin charge - minority spin charge. Used to impose a specific total electronic magnetization. If unspecified then tot_magnetization variable is ignored and the amount of electronic magnetization is determined during the self-consistent cycle.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.ecutwfc
documentation: kinetic energy cutoff (Ry) for wavefunctions

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.ecutrho
documentation: Kinetic energy cutoff (Ry) for charge density and potential For norm-conserving pseudopotential you should stick to the default value, you can reduce it by a little but it will introduce noise especially on forces and stress. Default: 4 * ecutwfc

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.ecutfock
documentation: Kinetic energy cutoff (Ry) for the exact exchange operator in EXX type calculations. By default this is the same as ecutrho but in some EXX calculations, a significant speed-up can be obtained by reducing ecutfock, at the expense of some loss in accuracy. Must be .gt. ecutwfc. Not implemented for stress calculation and for US-PP and PAW pseudopotentials.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.nr1
documentation: Three-dimensional FFT mesh (hard grid) for charge density (and scf potential). If not specified the grid is calculated based on the cutoff for charge density (see also ecutrho)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.nr2
documentation: Three-dimensional FFT mesh (hard grid) for charge density (and scf potential). If not specified the grid is calculated based on the cutoff for charge density (see also ecutrho)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.nr3
documentation: Three-dimensional FFT mesh (hard grid) for charge density (and scf potential). If not specified the grid is calculated based on the cutoff for charge density (see also ecutrho)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.nr1s
documentation: Three-dimensional mesh for wavefunction FFT and for the smooth part of charge density ( smooth grid ). Coincides with nr1, nr2, nr3 if ecutrho = 4 * ecutwfc ( default )

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.nr2s
documentation: Three-dimensional mesh for wavefunction FFT and for the smooth part of charge density ( smooth grid ). Coincides with nr1, nr2, nr3 if ecutrho = 4 * ecutwfc ( default )

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.nr3s
documentation: Three-dimensional mesh for wavefunction FFT and for the smooth part of charge density ( smooth grid ). Coincides with nr1, nr2, nr3 if ecutrho = 4 * ecutwfc ( default )

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.noinv
documentation: if (.TRUE.) disable the usage of k => -k symmetry (time reversal) in k-point generation

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.no_t_rev
documentation: if (.TRUE.) disable the usage of magnetic symmetry operations that consist in a rotation + time reversal.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.force_symmorphic
documentation: if (.TRUE.) force the symmetry group to be symmorphic by disabling symmetry operations having an associated fractionary translation

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.degauss_cond
documentation: value of the gaussian spreading (Ry) for brillouin-zone integration in the conduction manifold in a two-chemical potential calculation (twochem=.true.).

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.nelec_cond
documentation: Number of electrons placed in the conduction manifold in a two-chemical potential calculation (twochem=.true.). Of the total # of electrons nelec, nelec-nelec_cond will occupy the valence manifold and nelec_cond will be constrained in the conduction manifold.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.degauss
documentation: value of the gaussian spreading (Ry) for brillouin-zone integration in metals.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.sic_gamma
documentation: Strength of the gammaDFT potential.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.pol_type
documentation: Type of polaron in gammaDFT.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.sic_energy
documentation: Enable the calculation of the total energy in gammaDFT. When .true., a preliminary calculation is performed to calculate the electron density in the absence of the polaron. When .false., the total energy printed in output should not be considered. For structural relaxations, it is recommended to use .false. to avoid doubling the computational cost.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.sci_vb
documentation: Valence band shift (in eV) through self-consistent scissor operator. When performing gammaDFT calculations of polarons, the polaron level is not shifted.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.sci_cb
documentation: Conduction band band shift (in eV) through self-consistent scissor operator. When performing gammaDFT calculations of polarons, the polaron level is not shifted.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.noncolin
documentation: if .true. the program will perform a noncollinear calculation.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.input_dft
documentation: Exchange-correlation functional: eg 'PBE', 'BLYP' etc See Modules/funct.f90 for allowed values. Overrides the value read from pseudopotential files. Use with care and if you know what you are doing!

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.ace
documentation: Use Adaptively Compressed Exchange operator as in Lin Lin, J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2016, 12, 2242--2249, doi:10.1021/acs.jctc.6b00092

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.exx_fraction
documentation: Fraction of EXX for hybrid functional calculations. In the case of input_dft='PBE0', the default value is 0.25, while for input_dft='B3LYP' the exx_fraction default value is 0.20.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.screening_parameter
documentation: screening_parameter for HSE like hybrid functionals.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.x_gamma_extrapolation
documentation: Specific for EXX. If .true., extrapolate the G=0 term of the potential

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.ecutvcut
documentation: Reciprocal space cutoff for correcting Coulomb potential divergencies at small q vectors.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.nqx1
documentation: Three-dimensional mesh for q (k1-k2) sampling of the Fock operator (EXX). Can be smaller than the number of k-points.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.nqx2
documentation: Three-dimensional mesh for q (k1-k2) sampling of the Fock operator (EXX). Can be smaller than the number of k-points.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.nqx3
documentation: Three-dimensional mesh for q (k1-k2) sampling of the Fock operator (EXX). Can be smaller than the number of k-points.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.localization_thr
documentation: Overlap threshold over which the exchange integral over a pair of localized orbitals is included in the evaluation of EXX operator. Any value greater than 0.0 triggers the SCDM localization and the evaluation on EXX using the localized orbitals. Very small value of the threshold should yield the same result as the default EXX evaluation

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.dmft
documentation: If true, nscf calculation will exit in restart mode, scf calculation will restart from there if DMFT updates are provided as hdf5 archive. Scf calculation should be used only with electron_maxstep = 1.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.dmft_prefix
documentation: prepended to hdf5 archive: dmft_prefix.h5

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.ensemble_energies
documentation: If ensemble_energies = .true., an ensemble of xc energies is calculated non-selfconsistently for perturbed exchange-enhancement factors and LDA vs. PBE correlation ratios after each converged electronic ground state calculation.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.edir
documentation: The direction of the electric field or dipole correction is parallel to the bg(:,edir) reciprocal lattice vector, so the potential is constant in planes defined by FFT grid points; edir = 1, 2 or 3. Used only if tefield is .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.emaxpos
documentation: Position of the maximum of the saw-like potential along crystal axis edir, within the  unit cell (see below), 0 < emaxpos < 1 Used only if tefield is .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.eopreg
documentation: Zone in the unit cell where the saw-like potential decreases. ( see below, 0 < eopreg < 1 ). Used only if tefield is .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.angle1
documentation: The angle expressed in degrees between the initial magnetization and the z-axis. For noncollinear calculations only; index i runs over the atom types.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.angle2
documentation: The angle expressed in degrees between the projection of the initial magnetization on x-y plane and the x-axis. For noncollinear calculations only.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.lforcet
documentation: When starting a non collinear calculation using an existing density file from a collinear lsda calculation assumes previous density points in z direction and rotates it in the direction described by angle1 and angle2 variables for atomic type 1

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.constrained_magnetization
documentation: Used to perform constrained calculations in magnetic systems.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.lambda_
documentation: parameter used for constrained_magnetization calculations N.B.: if the scf calculation does not converge, try to reduce lambda to obtain convergence, then restart the run with a larger lambda

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.report
documentation: determines when atomic magnetic moments are printed on output

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.lspinorb
documentation: if .TRUE. the noncollinear code can use a pseudopotential with spin-orbit.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.assume_isolated
documentation: Used to perform calculation assuming the system to be isolated (a molecule or a cluster in a 3D supercell)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.esm_bc
documentation: If assume_isolated = 'esm', determines the boundary conditions used for either side of the slab.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.esm_w
documentation: If assume_isolated = 'esm', determines the position offset [in a.u.] of the start of the effective screening region, measured relative to the cell edge. (ESM region begins at z = +/- [L_z/2 + esm_w] ).

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.esm_efield
documentation: If assume_isolated = 'esm' and esm_bc = 'bc2', gives the magnitude of the electric field [Ry/a.u.] to be applied between semi-infinite ESM electrodes.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.esm_nfit
documentation: If assume_isolated = 'esm', gives the number of z-grid points for the polynomial fit along the cell edge.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.lgcscf
documentation: If .TRUE. perform a constant bias potential (constant-mu) calculation with Grand-Canonical SCF.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.gcscf_mu
documentation: The target Fermi energy (eV) of GC-SCF. One can start with appropriate total charge of the system by giving tot_charge

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.gcscf_conv_thr
documentation: Convergence threshold of Fermi energy (eV) for GC-SCF.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.gcscf_beta
documentation: Mixing factor for GC-SCF. Larger values are recommended, if systems with small DOS on Fermi surface as graphite.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.vdw_corr
documentation: Type of Van der Waals correction

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.london
documentation: OBSOLESCENT, same as vdw_corr='DFT-D'

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.london_s6
documentation: global scaling parameter for DFT-D. Default is good for PBE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.london_c6
documentation: atomic C6 coefficient of each atom type

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.london_rvdw
documentation: atomic vdw radii of each atom type

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.london_rcut
documentation: cutoff radius (a.u.) for dispersion interactions

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.dftd3_version
documentation: Version of Grimme implementation of Grimme-D3

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.dftd3_threebody
documentation: Turn three-body terms in Grimme-D3 on. If .false. two-body contributions only are computed, using two-body parameters of Grimme-D3. If dftd3_version=2, three-body contribution is always disabled.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.ts_vdw_econv_thr
documentation: Optional: controls the convergence of the vdW energy (and forces). The default value is a safe choice, likely too safe, but you do not gain much in increasing it

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.ts_vdw_isolated
documentation: Optional: set it to .TRUE. when computing the Tkatchenko-Scheffler vdW energy or the Many-Body dispersion (MBD) energy for an isolated (non-periodic) system.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.xdm
documentation: OBSOLESCENT, same as vdw_corr='xdm'

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.xdm_a1
documentation: Damping function parameter a1 (adimensional)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.xdm_a2
documentation: Damping function parameter a2 (angstrom)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.space_group
documentation: The number of the space group of the crystal, as given in the International Tables of Crystallography A (ITA)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.uniqueb
documentation: Used only for monoclinic lattices

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.origin_choice
documentation: Used only for space groups that in the ITA allow the use of two different origins

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.rhombohedral
documentation: Used only for rhombohedral space groups.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.zgate
documentation: used only if gate = .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.relaxz
documentation: used only if gate = .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.block
documentation: used only if gate = .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.block_1
documentation: used only if gate = .TRUE. and block = .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.block_2
documentation: used only if gate = .TRUE. and block = .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.block_height
documentation: used only if gate = .TRUE. and block = .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema.nextffield
documentation: Number of activated external ionic force fields.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.nat
documentation: number of atoms in the unit cell (ALL atoms, except if space_group is set, in which case, INEQUIVALENT atoms)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.ntyp
documentation: number of types of atoms in the unit cell

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.nbnd
documentation: Default: for an insulator, nbnd = number of valence bands (nbnd = # of electrons /2); for a metal, 20% more (minimum 4 more)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.nbnd_cond
documentation: Default: nbnd_cond = nbnd - # of electrons / 2 in the collinear case; nbnd_cond = nbnd - # of electrons in the noncollinear case.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.starting_charge
documentation: starting charge on atomic type 'i', to create starting potential with startingpot = 'atomic'.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.tot_magnetization
documentation: Total majority spin charge - minority spin charge. Used to impose a specific total electronic magnetization. If unspecified then tot_magnetization variable is ignored and the amount of electronic magnetization is determined during the self-consistent cycle.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.ecutwfc
documentation: kinetic energy cutoff (Ry) for wavefunctions

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.ecutrho
documentation: Kinetic energy cutoff (Ry) for charge density and potential For norm-conserving pseudopotential you should stick to the default value, you can reduce it by a little but it will introduce noise especially on forces and stress. Default: 4 * ecutwfc

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.ecutfock
documentation: Kinetic energy cutoff (Ry) for the exact exchange operator in EXX type calculations. By default this is the same as ecutrho but in some EXX calculations, a significant speed-up can be obtained by reducing ecutfock, at the expense of some loss in accuracy. Must be .gt. ecutwfc. Not implemented for stress calculation and for US-PP and PAW pseudopotentials.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.nr1
documentation: Three-dimensional FFT mesh (hard grid) for charge density (and scf potential). If not specified the grid is calculated based on the cutoff for charge density (see also ecutrho)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.nr2
documentation: Three-dimensional FFT mesh (hard grid) for charge density (and scf potential). If not specified the grid is calculated based on the cutoff for charge density (see also ecutrho)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.nr3
documentation: Three-dimensional FFT mesh (hard grid) for charge density (and scf potential). If not specified the grid is calculated based on the cutoff for charge density (see also ecutrho)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.nr1s
documentation: Three-dimensional mesh for wavefunction FFT and for the smooth part of charge density ( smooth grid ). Coincides with nr1, nr2, nr3 if ecutrho = 4 * ecutwfc ( default )

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.nr2s
documentation: Three-dimensional mesh for wavefunction FFT and for the smooth part of charge density ( smooth grid ). Coincides with nr1, nr2, nr3 if ecutrho = 4 * ecutwfc ( default )

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.nr3s
documentation: Three-dimensional mesh for wavefunction FFT and for the smooth part of charge density ( smooth grid ). Coincides with nr1, nr2, nr3 if ecutrho = 4 * ecutwfc ( default )

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.noinv
documentation: if (.TRUE.) disable the usage of k => -k symmetry (time reversal) in k-point generation

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.no_t_rev
documentation: if (.TRUE.) disable the usage of magnetic symmetry operations that consist in a rotation + time reversal.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.force_symmorphic
documentation: if (.TRUE.) force the symmetry group to be symmorphic by disabling symmetry operations having an associated fractionary translation

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.degauss_cond
documentation: value of the gaussian spreading (Ry) for brillouin-zone integration in the conduction manifold in a two-chemical potential calculation (twochem=.true.).

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.nelec_cond
documentation: Number of electrons placed in the conduction manifold in a two-chemical potential calculation (twochem=.true.). Of the total # of electrons nelec, nelec-nelec_cond will occupy the valence manifold and nelec_cond will be constrained in the conduction manifold.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.degauss
documentation: value of the gaussian spreading (Ry) for brillouin-zone integration in metals.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.sic_gamma
documentation: Strength of the gammaDFT potential.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.pol_type
documentation: Type of polaron in gammaDFT.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.sic_energy
documentation: Enable the calculation of the total energy in gammaDFT. When .true., a preliminary calculation is performed to calculate the electron density in the absence of the polaron. When .false., the total energy printed in output should not be considered. For structural relaxations, it is recommended to use .false. to avoid doubling the computational cost.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.sci_vb
documentation: Valence band shift (in eV) through self-consistent scissor operator. When performing gammaDFT calculations of polarons, the polaron level is not shifted.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.sci_cb
documentation: Conduction band band shift (in eV) through self-consistent scissor operator. When performing gammaDFT calculations of polarons, the polaron level is not shifted.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.noncolin
documentation: if .true. the program will perform a noncollinear calculation.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.input_dft
documentation: Exchange-correlation functional: eg 'PBE', 'BLYP' etc See Modules/funct.f90 for allowed values. Overrides the value read from pseudopotential files. Use with care and if you know what you are doing!

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.ace
documentation: Use Adaptively Compressed Exchange operator as in Lin Lin, J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2016, 12, 2242--2249, doi:10.1021/acs.jctc.6b00092

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.exx_fraction
documentation: Fraction of EXX for hybrid functional calculations. In the case of input_dft='PBE0', the default value is 0.25, while for input_dft='B3LYP' the exx_fraction default value is 0.20.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.screening_parameter
documentation: screening_parameter for HSE like hybrid functionals.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.x_gamma_extrapolation
documentation: Specific for EXX. If .true., extrapolate the G=0 term of the potential

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.ecutvcut
documentation: Reciprocal space cutoff for correcting Coulomb potential divergencies at small q vectors.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.nqx1
documentation: Three-dimensional mesh for q (k1-k2) sampling of the Fock operator (EXX). Can be smaller than the number of k-points.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.nqx2
documentation: Three-dimensional mesh for q (k1-k2) sampling of the Fock operator (EXX). Can be smaller than the number of k-points.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.nqx3
documentation: Three-dimensional mesh for q (k1-k2) sampling of the Fock operator (EXX). Can be smaller than the number of k-points.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.localization_thr
documentation: Overlap threshold over which the exchange integral over a pair of localized orbitals is included in the evaluation of EXX operator. Any value greater than 0.0 triggers the SCDM localization and the evaluation on EXX using the localized orbitals. Very small value of the threshold should yield the same result as the default EXX evaluation

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.dmft
documentation: If true, nscf calculation will exit in restart mode, scf calculation will restart from there if DMFT updates are provided as hdf5 archive. Scf calculation should be used only with electron_maxstep = 1.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.dmft_prefix
documentation: prepended to hdf5 archive: dmft_prefix.h5

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.ensemble_energies
documentation: If ensemble_energies = .true., an ensemble of xc energies is calculated non-selfconsistently for perturbed exchange-enhancement factors and LDA vs. PBE correlation ratios after each converged electronic ground state calculation.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.edir
documentation: The direction of the electric field or dipole correction is parallel to the bg(:,edir) reciprocal lattice vector, so the potential is constant in planes defined by FFT grid points; edir = 1, 2 or 3. Used only if tefield is .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.emaxpos
documentation: Position of the maximum of the saw-like potential along crystal axis edir, within the  unit cell (see below), 0 < emaxpos < 1 Used only if tefield is .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.eopreg
documentation: Zone in the unit cell where the saw-like potential decreases. ( see below, 0 < eopreg < 1 ). Used only if tefield is .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.angle1
documentation: The angle expressed in degrees between the initial magnetization and the z-axis. For noncollinear calculations only; index i runs over the atom types.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.angle2
documentation: The angle expressed in degrees between the projection of the initial magnetization on x-y plane and the x-axis. For noncollinear calculations only.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.lforcet
documentation: When starting a non collinear calculation using an existing density file from a collinear lsda calculation assumes previous density points in z direction and rotates it in the direction described by angle1 and angle2 variables for atomic type 1

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.constrained_magnetization
documentation: Used to perform constrained calculations in magnetic systems.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.lambda_
documentation: parameter used for constrained_magnetization calculations N.B.: if the scf calculation does not converge, try to reduce lambda to obtain convergence, then restart the run with a larger lambda

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.report
documentation: determines when atomic magnetic moments are printed on output

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.lspinorb
documentation: if .TRUE. the noncollinear code can use a pseudopotential with spin-orbit.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.assume_isolated
documentation: Used to perform calculation assuming the system to be isolated (a molecule or a cluster in a 3D supercell)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.esm_bc
documentation: If assume_isolated = 'esm', determines the boundary conditions used for either side of the slab.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.esm_w
documentation: If assume_isolated = 'esm', determines the position offset [in a.u.] of the start of the effective screening region, measured relative to the cell edge. (ESM region begins at z = +/- [L_z/2 + esm_w] ).

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.esm_efield
documentation: If assume_isolated = 'esm' and esm_bc = 'bc2', gives the magnitude of the electric field [Ry/a.u.] to be applied between semi-infinite ESM electrodes.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.esm_nfit
documentation: If assume_isolated = 'esm', gives the number of z-grid points for the polynomial fit along the cell edge.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.lgcscf
documentation: If .TRUE. perform a constant bias potential (constant-mu) calculation with Grand-Canonical SCF.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.gcscf_mu
documentation: The target Fermi energy (eV) of GC-SCF. One can start with appropriate total charge of the system by giving tot_charge

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.gcscf_conv_thr
documentation: Convergence threshold of Fermi energy (eV) for GC-SCF.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.gcscf_beta
documentation: Mixing factor for GC-SCF. Larger values are recommended, if systems with small DOS on Fermi surface as graphite.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.vdw_corr
documentation: Type of Van der Waals correction

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.london
documentation: OBSOLESCENT, same as vdw_corr='DFT-D'

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.london_s6
documentation: global scaling parameter for DFT-D. Default is good for PBE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.london_c6
documentation: atomic C6 coefficient of each atom type

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.london_rvdw
documentation: atomic vdw radii of each atom type

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.london_rcut
documentation: cutoff radius (a.u.) for dispersion interactions

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.dftd3_version
documentation: Version of Grimme implementation of Grimme-D3

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.dftd3_threebody
documentation: Turn three-body terms in Grimme-D3 on. If .false. two-body contributions only are computed, using two-body parameters of Grimme-D3. If dftd3_version=2, three-body contribution is always disabled.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.ts_vdw_econv_thr
documentation: Optional: controls the convergence of the vdW energy (and forces). The default value is a safe choice, likely too safe, but you do not gain much in increasing it

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.ts_vdw_isolated
documentation: Optional: set it to .TRUE. when computing the Tkatchenko-Scheffler vdW energy or the Many-Body dispersion (MBD) energy for an isolated (non-periodic) system.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.xdm
documentation: OBSOLESCENT, same as vdw_corr='xdm'

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.xdm_a1
documentation: Damping function parameter a1 (adimensional)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.xdm_a2
documentation: Damping function parameter a2 (angstrom)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.space_group
documentation: The number of the space group of the crystal, as given in the International Tables of Crystallography A (ITA)

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.uniqueb
documentation: Used only for monoclinic lattices

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.origin_choice
documentation: Used only for space groups that in the ITA allow the use of two different origins

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.rhombohedral
documentation: Used only for rhombohedral space groups.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.zgate
documentation: used only if gate = .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.relaxz
documentation: used only if gate = .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.block
documentation: used only if gate = .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.block_1
documentation: used only if gate = .TRUE. and block = .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.block_2
documentation: used only if gate = .TRUE. and block = .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.block_height
documentation: used only if gate = .TRUE. and block = .TRUE.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.SystemSchema1.nextffield
documentation: Number of activated external ionic force fields.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.electron_maxstep
documentation: maximum number of iterations in a scf step. If exact exchange is active, this will affect the inner loops.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.exx_maxstep
documentation: maximum number of outer iterations in a scf calculation with exact exchange.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.scf_must_converge
documentation: If .false. do not stop molecular dynamics or ionic relaxation when electron_maxstep is reached. Use with care.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.adaptive_thr
documentation: If .TRUE. this turns on the use of an adaptive conv_thr for the inner scf loops when using EXX.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.conv_thr_init
documentation: When adaptive_thr = .TRUE. this is the convergence threshold used for the first scf cycle.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.conv_thr_multi
documentation: When adaptive_thr = .TRUE. the convergence threshold for each scf cycle is given by: max( conv_thr, conv_thr_multi * dexx )

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.mixing_beta
documentation: mixing factor for self-consistency

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.mixing_ndim
documentation: number of iterations used in mixing scheme

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.mixing_fixed_ns
documentation: For DFT+U : number of iterations with fixed ns ( ns is the atomic density appearing in the Hubbard term ).

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.diago_thr_init
documentation: Convergence threshold (ethr) for iterative diagonalization (the check is on eigenvalue convergence).

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.diago_cg_maxiter
documentation: For conjugate gradient diagonalization:  max number of iterations

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.diago_ppcg_maxiter
documentation: For ppcg diagonalization:  max number of iterations

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.diago_david_ndim
documentation: For Davidson diagonalization: dimension of workspace (number of wavefunction packets, at least 2 needed).

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.diago_rmm_ndim
documentation: For RMM-DIIS diagonalization: dimension of workspace (number of wavefunction packets, at least 2 needed).

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.diago_rmm_conv
documentation: If .TRUE., RMM-DIIS is performed up to converge. If .FALSE., RMM-DIIS is performed only once.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.diago_gs_nblock
documentation: For RMM-DIIS diagonalization: blocking size of Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.diago_full_acc
documentation: If .TRUE. all the empty states are diagonalized at the same level of accuracy of the occupied ones. Otherwise the empty states are diagonalized using a larger threshold (this should not affect total energy, forces, and other ground-state properties).

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.efield
documentation: Amplitude of the finite electric field (in Ry a.u.; 1 a.u. = 36.3609*10^10 V/m). Used only if lelfield==.TRUE. and if k-points (K_POINTS card) are not automatic.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.tqr
documentation: If .true., use a real-space algorithm for augmentation charges of ultrasoft pseudopotentials and PAWsets. Faster but numerically less accurate than the default G-space algorithm. Use with care and after testing!

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.ElectronsSchema.real_space
documentation: If .true., exploit real-space localization to compute matrix elements for nonlocal projectors. Faster and in principle better scaling than the default G-space algorithm, but numerically less accurate, may lead to some loss of translational invariance. Use with care and after testing!

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.IonsSchema.pot_extrapolation
documentation: Used to extrapolate the potential from preceding ionic steps.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.IonsSchema.wfc_extrapolation
documentation: Used to extrapolate the wavefunctions from preceding ionic steps.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.IonsSchema.remove_rigid_rot
documentation: This keyword is useful when simulating the dynamics and/or the thermodynamics of an isolated system. If set to true the total torque of the internal forces is set to zero by adding new forces that compensate the spurious interaction with the periodic images. This allows for the use of smaller supercells.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.IonsSchema.tempw
documentation: Starting temperature (Kelvin) in MD runs target temperature for most thermostats.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.IonsSchema.tolp
documentation: Tolerance for velocity rescaling. Velocities are rescaled if the run-averaged and target temperature differ more than tolp.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.IonsSchema.refold_pos
documentation: This keyword applies only in the case of molecular dynamics or damped dynamics. If true the ions are refolded at each step into the supercell.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.IonsSchema.upscale
documentation: Max reduction factor for conv_thr during structural optimization conv_thr is automatically reduced when the relaxation approaches convergence so that forces are still accurate, but conv_thr will not be reduced to less that conv_thr / upscale.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.IonsSchema.bfgs_ndim
documentation: Number of old forces and displacements vectors used in the PULAY mixing of the residual vectors obtained on the basis of the inverse hessian matrix given by the BFGS algorithm.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.third_party.file.applications.espresso.7.2.pw.x.IonsSchema.trust_radius_max

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.job.QuantumEspressoArgumentsSchema.nimage
documentation: Processors can be divided into different `images`, each corresponding to a different self-consistent or linear-response calculation, loosely coupled to others.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.job.QuantumEspressoArgumentsSchema.npools
documentation: Each image can be subpartitioned into `pools`, each taking care of a group of k-points.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.job.QuantumEspressoArgumentsSchema.nband
documentation: Each pool is subpartitioned into `band groups`, each taking care of a group of Kohn-Sham orbitals (also called bands, or wavefunctions).

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.job.QuantumEspressoArgumentsSchema.ntg
documentation: In order to allow good parallelization of the 3D FFT when the number of processors exceeds the number of FFT planes, FFTs on Kohn-Sham states are redistributed to `task` groups so that each group can process several wavefunctions at the same time.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.job.QuantumEspressoArgumentsSchema.ndiag
documentation: A further level of parallelization, independent on PW or k-point parallelization, is the parallelization of subspace diagonalization / iterative orthonormalization. Both operations required the diagonalization of arrays whose dimension is the number of Kohn-Sham states (or a small multiple of it). All such arrays are distributed block-like across the `linear-algebra group`, a subgroup of the pool of processors, organized in a square 2D grid. As a consequence the number of processors in the linear-algebra group is given by n2, where n is an integer; n2 must be smaller than the number of processors in the PW group. The diagonalization is then performed in parallel using standard linear algebra operations.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.software_directory.modeling.espresso.arguments.QuantumEspressoArgumentsSchema.nimage
documentation: Processors can be divided into different `images`, each corresponding to a different self-consistent or linear-response calculation, loosely coupled to others.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.software_directory.modeling.espresso.arguments.QuantumEspressoArgumentsSchema.npools
documentation: Each image can be subpartitioned into `pools`, each taking care of a group of k-points.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.software_directory.modeling.espresso.arguments.QuantumEspressoArgumentsSchema.nband
documentation: Each pool is subpartitioned into `band groups`, each taking care of a group of Kohn-Sham orbitals (also called bands, or wavefunctions).

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.software_directory.modeling.espresso.arguments.QuantumEspressoArgumentsSchema.ntg
documentation: In order to allow good parallelization of the 3D FFT when the number of processors exceeds the number of FFT planes, FFTs on Kohn-Sham states are redistributed to `task` groups so that each group can process several wavefunctions at the same time.

class_variables.name: mat3ra.esse.models.software_directory.modeling.espresso.arguments.QuantumEspressoArgumentsSchema.ndiag
documentation: A further level of parallelization, independent on PW or k-point parallelization, is the parallelization of subspace diagonalization / iterative orthonormalization. Both operations required the diagonalization of arrays whose dimension is the number of Kohn-Sham states (or a small multiple of it). All such arrays are distributed block-like across the `linear-algebra group`, a subgroup of the pool of processors, organized in a square 2D grid. As a consequence the number of processors in the linear-algebra group is given by n2, where n is an integer; n2 must be smaller than the number of processors in the PW group. The diagonalization is then performed in parallel using standard linear algebra operations.
